item.title: House GOP Leaders Unveil New Spy Powers Plan, Seek Party Unity
item.description: House Republicans unveiled their latest plan to extend a US spy powers tool after a week of rebellion from privacy hawks forced leadership to settle for a 10-day extension as they worked through negotiations.

item.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.5 to Field Tasks With Limited Instructions
item.description: OpenAI is introducing an artificial intelligence model that’s intended to be better at completing work without much direction, part of a push to keep pace with rivals like Anthropic PBC in courting business customers.

item.title: Oil Jumps as Iran Tensions Simmer With Hormuz Flows Blocked
item.description: Oil jumped amid fresh signs of escalation in the Iran war, dimming prospects for a imminent resumption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Amtrak Sues Metro-North Over Denied Request to Run Work Trains
item.description: Amtrak is seeking a preliminary injunction against Metro-North Railroad that would allow its non-revenue trains to run between New York City’s northern suburbs and Connecticut.

item.title: Meta Tells Staff It Will Cut 10% of Jobs in Push for Efficiency
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. plans to cut 10% of workers, or roughly 8,000 employees, in an effort to boost efficiency and offset its heavy spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Blackstone’s Secondaries Unit Hits $100 Billion as Demand Grows
item.description: Blackstone Inc.’s secondaries business reached $100 billion of assets under management in the first quarter, making it one of the biggest players in the market for selling secondhand stakes.

item.title: White House Reviewing SEC Bid to Ease Share-Offering Disclosures
item.description: The White House is reviewing new US Securities and Exchange Commission rules that would make it easier for companies to go public or raise capital by easing disclosures for new offerings and speeding up the registration process.

item.title: Avis Stock Collapse Sparks Plunge in Dow Transports It Lifted
item.description: A gauge of US transportation stocks is heading for its worst two-day stretch since last spring’s tariff selloff.

item.title: Trump’s Emergency Oil Sails to Europe as War Upends Energy Flows
item.description: A massive release of oil from US emergency reserves that has started flowing to American refiners is also supplying fuelmakers in Europe, and potentially in Asia, as the Iran war upends global energy markets.

item.title: ADP Is Said Planning to Reduce Stake in India’s GMR Airports
item.description: ADP, operator of the main Paris airports, is planning to reduce its stake in India’s GMR Airports Ltd., people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Siemens Energy Raises Outlook on Strong AI-Driven Demand
item.description: Gas-turbine and energy-equipment maker Siemens Energy AG raised its outlook for the fiscal year 2026, citing strong demand.

item.title: US Seeks to Halt US AI Model ‘Exploitation’ by Chinese Rivals
item.description: The Trump administration unveiled measures aimed at preventing Chinese developers from improperly using leading American AI models to build a rival generation of chatbots, marking the first major US response to Silicon Valley companies’ complaints that China is piggybacking on their success.

item.title: Key Takeaways on Complaints Inside Alex Cooper’s Podcast Empire
item.description: The host of Call Her Daddy has been grappling with multiple challenges inside her business, some of them brought about by her husband.

item.title: Sheinbaum Points to Local Officials After CIA Agent Deaths
item.description: Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum sought to ease tensions with the US after the death of CIA agents following a recent drug lab bust in northern Mexico, blaming an opposition state government for failing to adhere to proper security protocols.

item.title: Rogers’ Spending Cut Plan Prompts Two Analysts to Boost Ratings
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc.’s decision to pull back on capital spending and boost free cash flow this year prompted at least two analysts to raise their recommendation on the telecommunications firm after the company released its first-quarter results Wednesday.

item.title: Dow CEO Says Petrochemical Supply Disruptions to Persist in 2026
item.description: Dow Inc., one of the world’s largest producers of petrochemicals, expects supply disruptions tied to the war in Iran to persist throughout the remainder of 2026, according to Chief Executive Officer Jim Fitterling.

item.title: Uruguay Finance Chief Says Pensions Will Stay in Private Hands
item.description: Uruguay’s pension savings will continue to be managed by the private sector under the government’s planned overhaul of the social security system, Finance Minister Gabriel Oddone said.

item.title: Treasuries Headed for Tightest Monthly Trading Range Since 2020
item.description: US Treasury benchmark yields have been locked in the narrowest range since the depths of the coronavirus pandemic, as investors fatigued by conflicting geopolitical headlines search for direction.

item.title: Microsoft Offers Voluntary Retirement to About 7% of US Workers
item.description: Microsoft Corp. is offering voluntary retirement to thousands of its employees in the US.

item.title: Shale Bosses See ‘Chaos’ From Iran War in Anonymous Fed Survey
item.description: US shale executives are frustrated with the “chaos” in oil markets that has come from the Middle East conflict, according to the latest energy survey from the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

item.title: ECB’s Nagel Says Trump’s Fed Attacks Caused Flight to Safety
item.description: President Donald Trump’s repeated attacks on the Federal Reserve are backfiring as they cause distrust in US institutions and a flight from US assets and the dollar, European Central Bank Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said.

item.title: Too Tipsy to Drive? German Suppliers Have In-Car Sensor for That
item.description: Volkswagen AG, Stellantis NV and Mercedes-Benz Group AG models may soon feature touchscreens able to test blood alcohol levels for drivers.

item.title: CAAT Pension Earns 8.4% as Stock Gains Outweigh Weak PE Returns
item.description: CAAT Pension Plan earned 8.4% last year as buoyant stock performance outweighed soft returns in the fund’s private market portfolio.

item.title: Czech Utility Giant CEZ Plans Asset Split Before Possible Buyout
item.description: Czech power utility CEZ AS outlined a plan to split its assets, a move that could pave the way for the government to obtain full ownership of its electricity production in a long-planned buyout.

item.title: Pimco Privately Lends $10 Billion to Gulf in Wartime Bond Deals
item.description: As Persian Gulf states build cash buffers to deal with any potential economic fallout from the Iran war, one large buyer has stepped in: Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: Avis Shares Plunge 62% in Two Days in Crashing Halt to Rally
item.description: A searing surge in the shares of Avis Budget Group Inc. has hit a wall, with the stock plunging over 62% in just two days.

item.title: AI Junk-Bond Binge Brings Rare Early Repayments to Sweeten Deals
item.description: As a more than $20 billion borrowing frenzy to build out data centers descended on the junk-bond market this year, some issuers offered up a rare sweetener: an early cash payback.

item.title: Apollo’s Ingenico Starts Talks With Lenders on ‘Untenable’ Debt
item.description: French payments firm Ingenico, backed by Apollo Global Management, has started debt talks with creditors as it struggles to keep up with its interest bill.

item.title: Banks Kick Off Talks on Lone Star’s €1.5 Billion Lonza Unit Debt
item.description: Banks are preparing to sell €1.5 billion ($1.75 billion) of debt financing backing Lone Star Funds’ acquisition of Lonza Group AG’s capsules and health ingredients business.

item.title: US Business Activity Expands While Prices Rise Most Since 2022
item.description: US business activity picked up in April, fueled by the strongest manufacturing growth in nearly four years as war-related supply disruptions prompted a scramble for supplies.
